steps.title: Block the form
steps.description: Lightly sketch a rounded shape — a little wider than tall, slightly asymmetric. Real apples are rarely perfectly round.

steps.title: Shape the top and bottom
steps.description: Draw a small inward curve at the top of the outline — a gentle dip in the center — to suggest where the stem sits. Add a shallower matching curve at the bottom.

steps.title: Outline with confidence
steps.description: Draw the main outline with a single committed stroke. Slight wobble is fine — apples are organic.

steps.title: Add the stem
steps.description: Add a short stem rising from the top dimple, slightly tilted to one side.

steps.title: Add a leaf
steps.description: Sketch a small leaf next to the stem with a central vein. Two short strokes meeting at a point gives you the leaf shape.

steps.title: Hint at form
steps.description: Add a small curved line near the top dimple to suggest depth where the stem meets the apple.
